name: RedAlert MCP Server - REST API E2E Test Plan
description: Comprehensive end-to-end test cases for all 8 REST API MCP tools

testCases.description: Get current active alerts with no params

validate.description: Should return object with alert type keys mapping to city arrays. Keys (if any) should be valid alert type strings, values should be arrays of strings.

testCases.description: Verify response structure has valid alert type keys

validate.description: Any keys present must be from the known alert types: missiles, earthQuake, tsunami, hostileAircraftIntrusion, hazardousMaterials, terroristInfiltration, newsFlash, unconventionalWarfare, radiologicalEvent, generalAlert, and their drill variants. Values must be string arrays.

testCases.description: Verify active alerts returns consistent results on repeated calls

validate.description: Two rapid sequential calls should return the same structure (keys match). This validates caching/consistency.
Response must contain totals.range, totals.last24h, totals.last7d, totals.last30d (all numbers), plus uniqueCities, uniqueZones, uniqueOrigins (all numbers).
Response must contain topCities[] with city/zone/count, topZones[] with zone/count, topOrigins[] with origin/count, and peak with period/count.
Response must contain totals.range reflecting alerts only within the specified week. totals.range should be a non-negative number.
Response must contain topCities array with at most 3 items, all related to gaza origin. totals should reflect gaza-only alerts.

testCases.description: Summary with timeline grouped by day

validate.description: Response must include timeline[] with period and count fields. Each period should represent a day bucket.
Response must contain data[] array with city, cityZone, count fields, and pagination object with total, limit, offset, hasMore.
Response data[] should only contain cities whose names partially match 'תל אביב'. Each entry must have city, cityZone, count.
Response data[] should have at most 3 entries. pagination.limit should be 3, pagination.offset should be 0.
Each entry in data[] must include translations (object with en, ru, ar keys) and lat/lng (numbers or null).

testCases.description: City stats with origin filter and date range

validate.description: Response should contain city stats filtered by lebanon origin within the date range. data[] should have at most 5 entries.
Response must contain data[] with entries having id, timestamp, type, origin, and cities[] (each with id and name). Pagination object must be present.
Response data[] should contain alerts related to cities matching 'אשקלון'. Post-processed cities[] in each alert should only include matching cities.
All entries in data[] must have type equal to 'missiles' (or the API's equivalent category string). At most 10 results.

testCases.description: History with date range and sorting

validate.description: All timestamps must be within 2023-10-07 to 2023-10-08. Results should be in ascending chronological order.

testCases.description: History with cityName exact match and include translations

validate.description: Post-processed cities[] should only contain the city 'שדרות'. Each city entry should have translations object.
Each city in data[].cities[] should include lat, lng (numbers or null) and polygons (object or null).
Response must contain data[] with category and count fields, totalAlerts number, and pagination object.
Response data[] should have entries grouped by origin (e.g. gaza, lebanon). Each entry has category (origin name) and count.

testCases.description: Distribution grouped by category with date range

validate.description: Response data[] should reflect category distribution within the specified date range. totalAlerts should be sum of all counts.

testCases.description: Distribution with origin filter and sorting

validate.description: Response data[] should have at most 5 entries sorted by count descending. All data should be filtered to gaza origin.
Response data[] should be sorted alphabetically by category name in ascending order.

testCases.description: Search shelters with lat/lon (Tel Aviv center)

validate.description: Response must have success=true, count number, and results[] with id, address, city, lat, lon, distance_m, distance_km, wheelchair_accessible fields.

testCases.description: Search shelters by city name only (auto-resolve coordinates)

validate.description: Should auto-resolve city to coordinates and return shelters. success=true, results[] should have valid shelter entries.
All results[].distance_km should be <= 0.5. success=true.
All results[] should have wheelchair_accessible=true. success=true.

testCases.description: Search shelters with no lat/lon and no city (error case)

validate.description: Should return an error indicating either lat/lon or city name must be provided.
Response must contain data[] with id, name, zone fields and pagination object. Default limit should return up to 100 entries.
data[] should only contain cities whose names partially match 'חיפה'. Each entry must have id, name, zone.
All entries in data[] should have zone matching 'גוש דן'. At most 10 results.
Each entry in data[] must include translations (object), lat/lng (number or null), and countdown (number or null).
pagination.offset should be 10, pagination.limit should be 5. data[] should have at most 5 entries. Results should differ from offset=0.
Should return a successful response indicating the API is healthy. Response should be parseable JSON.
Health check should respond within a reasonable time. Validate response is not an error.
